title: Taking about Classical Music in Eva again
display_title: Taking about Classical Music in Eva again
wikitext:
[]: Taking about Classical Music in Eva again...             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Sun Apr 18, 2010 8:24 am Well, does anyone have been studied or thought about why a particular piece would be chosen for the NGE : Death and EoE? For me, it's like this... Jesu,joy of human desiring : make you feel peace, slient and think about the world of Shinji in a deeper level... Air and Canon : I can't say sadness, just sigh for some events that people in Evangelion not deserve for... I can only remember the strangling scene in EoE and that world surrounding Shinji and Asuka when I listening Air...epsecially the red main color in that scene BWV1007 Cello :　A good introductory music to describe the world of Evangelion for someone who are new to this series....I'm the one of it. Beethoven Symphony no.9: A song that human wish for happiness... maybe it's a present for Shinji...just thinking about it That's all my temporary thoughts based on my Trinity Performer Cert Level knowledge since I have played piano for 10 yrs already...I also think that music in NGE is a great 'bond" between myself and Evangelion... Anybody know which piece of Dvorak used in Death ?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Apr 18, 2010 9:17 pm Anno often uses a technique called counterpoint where the music and narrative are disconjunctive. A famous example in film might be in Kubrick's A Clockwork Orange where Alex does Singin' in the Rain during a rape scene. I've often thought of doing a breakdown of all the occurrences in NGE and how it's used each time. I've always actually felt the Hallelujah Chorus to a mindrape was particularly perverse (but effective!). Beethoven's 9th works on many levels; much like that entire episode. On the one hand, it's totally apropos. It's huge, epic, dramatic music fitting a huge, epic, dramatic moment in the series. Sometimes the music synchs with the narrative ("Cherub stands at heaven's gate" or something like when Kaworu pauses at the door to Lilith). But it also runs in counterpoint with the idea of it being an Ode to Joy played out to the moment which finally sends Shinji into his mental breakdown and abject depression. I also love the beauty of air played out to Ritsuko's murder and 02's bloody MPE battle. Then there's another brilliant moment with Komm Susser Tod - a sad song with a pop beat - played out to the visually striking, apocalyptic 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Apr 18, 2010 9:23 pm It's basically Anno's take on usage of diagetic and non-diagetic sound in film. Fir a similar film that uses this pattern with classical/baroque period music, I'd suggest Amadeus . Also, +1 to Jimbo's post.

[]:             Quote Post by edmundasBakashinji » Sun Apr 18, 2010 10:32 pm ran1 wrote: It's basically Anno's take on usage of diagetic and non-diagetic sound in film. Fir a similar film that uses this pattern with classical/baroque period music, I'd suggest Amadeus . Also, +1 to Jimbo's post.
